title: benzoin oxyphyllum
intro: Benzoin oxyphyllum, also known as Styrax tonkinensis, is a type of tree native to Southeast Asia, specifically in Vietnam and Laos, which is valued for its aromatic resin. The plant's medicinal properties lie in its ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities, which contribute to its use in traditional medicine. The resin of the benzoin oxyphyllum plant has been used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems, providing relief from pain, inflammation, and infection. Herbal preparations of benzoin oxyphyllum resin include topical creams, ointments, and salves, as well as teas and infusions, which can be used to promote wound healing and soothe respiratory issues.
